generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Generate and initialize AIDE database if it doesn't exist
AIDE_DB="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz"
if [ ! -f "$AIDE_DB" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$AIDE_DB")"
    aide --init
    mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz "$AIDE_DB"
fi

# Ensure AIDE is configured to use the generated database
sed -i 's/^database_out.*/database_out = file:\/\/\/var\/lib\/aide\/aide.db.gz/' /etc/aide.conf

# Set correct permissions for the AIDE database
chown root:root "$AIDE_DB"
chmod 600 "$AIDE_DB"

# Ensure AIDE is enabled and running
systemctl enable aidecheck.timer
systemctl start aidecheck.timer

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Generate and initialize AIDE database if it doesn't exist
AIDE_DB="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz"
if [ ! -f "$AIDE_DB" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$AIDE_DB")"
    aide --init
    mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz "$AIDE_DB"
fi

# Ensure AIDE is configured to use the generated database
sed -i 's/^database_out.*/database_out = file:\/\/\/var\/lib\/aide\/aide.db.gz/' /etc/aide.conf

# Set correct permissions for the AIDE database
chown root:root "$AIDE_DB"
chmod 600 "$AIDE_DB"

# Ensure AIDE is enabled and running
systemctl enable aidecheck.timer
systemctl start aidecheck.timer
```

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit tools to be monitored by AIDE
audit_tools=(
    /usr/bin/ausearch
    /usr/sbin/aureport
    /sbin/auditctl
    /sbin/audispd
    /sbin/auditd
)

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Installing..."
    sudo yum install -y aide
fi

# Backup the original AIDE configuration file
sudo cp -n /etc/aide.conf /etc/aide.conf.bak

# Function to add or update entries in AIDE configuration
update_aide_config() {
    local tool_path="$1"
    if ! grep -q "^$tool_path" /etc/aide.conf; then
        echo "$tool_path R, p+i+n+u+g+s+b+m+c+t" | sudo tee -a /etc/aide.conf > /dev/null
    fi
}

# Register each audit tool with AIDE for monitoring
for tool in "${audit_tools[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$tool" ]; then
        update_aide_config "$tool"
    else
        echo "Warning: $tool does not exist and will not be monitored by AIDE."
    fi
done

# Update the AIDE database with the new configuration
sudo aide --init

# Ensure AIDE is set to run on boot
if ! systemctl is-enabled aidecheck.service &> /dev/null; then
    sudo systemctl enable aidecheck.service
fi

echo "AIDE has been configured to monitor audit tools."

    /sbin/auditctl
    /sbin/audispd
    /sbin/auditd
)

fi

# Backup the original AIDE configuration file

# Function to add or update entries in AIDE configuration

    local tool_path="$1"
    if ! grep -q "^$tool_path" /etc/aide.conf; then
        echo "$tool_path R, p+i+n+u+g+s+b+m+c+t" | sudo tee -a /etc/aide.conf > /dev/null
    fi
}

# Register each audit tool with AIDE for monitoring

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Installing..."
    sudo yum install -y aide
fi

# Configure AIDE to send email on scan completion
if [ ! -f /etc/aide.conf.d/99email ]; then
    cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/aide.conf.d/99email
report_url = mailto:root@localhost
EOF
fi

# Ensure the cron job for AIDE is set up
CRON_JOB="0 5 * * * /usr/sbin/aide --check"
if ! crontab -l | grep -q "$CRON_JOB"; then
    (crontab -l; echo "$CRON_JOB") | crontab -
fi

# Ensure mailx is installed for sending emails
if ! command -v mail &> /dev/null; then
    echo "mailx is not installed. Installing..."
    sudo yum install -y mailx
fi

# Configure mail to use the local sendmail service
if [ ! -f /etc/mail.rc ]; then
    cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/mail.rc
set sendmail=/usr/sbin/sendmail
EOF
fi

echo "AIDE configuration for email reporting is complete."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Installing..."
    sudo yum install -y aide
fi

# Configure AIDE to send email on scan completion
if [ ! -f /etc/aide.conf.d/99email ]; then
    cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/aide.conf.d/99email
report_url = mailto:root@localhost
EOF
fi

# Ensure the cron job for AIDE is set up
CRON_JOB="0 5 * * * /usr/sbin/aide --check"
if ! crontab -l | grep -q "$CRON_JOB"; then
    (crontab -l; echo "$CRON_JOB") | crontab -
fi

# Ensure mailx is installed for sending emails
if ! command -v mail &> /dev/null; then
    echo "mailx is not installed. Installing..."
    sudo yum install -y mailx
fi

# Configure mail to use the local sendmail service
if [ ! -f /etc/mail.rc ]; then
    cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/mail.rc
set sendmail=/usr/sbin/sendmail
EOF
fi

echo "AIDE configuration for email reporting is complete."
```

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Define the path to the AIDE configuration file
AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Create a backup of the original AIDE configuration file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "${AIDE_CONF}.bak" ]; then
    cp "$AIDE_CONF" "${AIDE_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Function to add ACL verification to a ruleset
add_acl_to_ruleset() {
    local ruleset="$1"
    # Check if the ruleset already includes ACL verification
    if grep -q "^$ruleset.*acl$" "$AIDE_CONF"; then
        echo "ACL verification is already enabled for $ruleset."
    else
        # Add ACL verification to the ruleset
        sed -i "/^$ruleset/s/$/ acl/" "$AIDE_CONF"
        echo "Added ACL verification to $ruleset."
    fi
}

# List of common AIDE rulesets to modify
RULESETS=("ALL" "BINARIES" "TEXT" "SCRIPTS" "CONFIG")

# Apply ACL verification to each ruleset
for RULESET in "${RULESETS[@]}"; do
    add_acl_to_ruleset "$RULESET"
done

# Reload AIDE configuration
aide --init > /dev/null 2>&1

echo "AIDE configuration updated to include ACL verification for all specified rulesets."

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Backup original AIDE configuration
cp /etc/aide.conf /etc/aide.conf.bak

# Function to enable extended attribute checking in a ruleset
enable_xattrs_in_ruleset() {
    local ruleset="$1"
    sed -i "/^$ruleset/ s/\(ALL=\)/\1+xattrs/" /etc/aide.conf
}

# Define AIDE rulesets that need xattr checks
declare -a rulesets=("FIPSR" "LOGFILES" "BINARIES" "SCRIPTS" "CONFIG" "DATABASE")

# Apply extended attribute checking to each defined ruleset
for ruleset in "${rulesets[@]}"; do
    if grep -q "^$ruleset=" /etc/aide.conf; then
        enable_xattrs_in_ruleset "$ruleset"
    fi
done

# Initialize AIDE database with the updated configuration
aide --init

# Move the new database to the default location
mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz

# Ensure AIDE cron job is enabled for regular checks
systemctl enable aide.timer
systemctl start aide.timer

echo "AIDE configuration updated to include extended attribute checking."

# Function to enable extended attribute checking in a ruleset
enable_xattrs_in_ruleset() {
    local ruleset="$1"
    sed -i "/^$ruleset/ s/\(ALL=\)/\1+xattrs/" /etc/aide.conf
}

# Define AIDE rulesets that need xattr checks
declare -a rulesets=("FIPSR" "LOGFILES" "BINARIES" "SCRIPTS" "CONFIG" "DATABASE")

# Apply extended attribute checking to each defined ruleset
for ruleset in "${rulesets[@]}"; do
    if grep -q "^$ruleset=" /etc/aide.conf; then

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure FIPS mode is enabled
if ! fips-mode-setup --enable; then
    echo "Failed to enable FIPS mode."
    exit 1
fi

# Verify FIPS mode is active
if ! fips-mode-setup --check | grep -q "FIPS mode is enabled"; then
    echo "FIPS mode is not enabled."
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the system reboots into FIPS mode by setting the kernel parameter
if ! grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="fips=1" --title="RHEL 8 with FIPS"; then
    echo "Failed to update GRUB configuration for FIPS mode."
    exit 1
fi

# Verify the GRUB configuration includes the fips=1 parameter
if ! grep -q "fips=1" /etc/default/grub; then
    echo "GRUB configuration does not include fips=1."
    exit 1
fi

# Update GRUB to apply changes
if ! grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg; then
    echo "Failed to update GRUB configuration file."
    exit 1
fi

echo "System is now configured to operate in FIPS mode and will remain so across reboots."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure FIPS mode is enabled
if ! fips-mode-setup --enable; then
    echo "Failed to enable FIPS mode."
    exit 1
fi

# Verify FIPS mode is active
if ! fips-mode-setup --check | grep -q "FIPS mode is enabled"; then
    echo "FIPS mode is not enabled."
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the system reboots into FIPS mode by setting the kernel parameter
if ! grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="fips=1" --title="RHEL 8 with FIPS"; then
    echo "Failed to update GRUB configuration for FIPS mode."
    exit 1
fi

# Verify the GRUB configuration includes the fips=1 parameter
if ! grep -q "fips=1" /etc/default/grub; then
    echo "GRUB configuration does not include fips=1."
    exit 1
fi

# Update GRUB to apply changes
if ! grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg; then
    echo "Failed to update GRUB configuration file."
    exit 1
fi

echo "System is now configured to operate in FIPS mode and will remain so across reboots."
```

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the system-wide cryptographic policy is set to default-strength
update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT

# Verify that the policy has been applied correctly
if update-crypto-policies --show | grep -q "DEFAULT"; then
    echo "Cryptographic policy set to DEFAULT successfully."
else
    echo "Failed to set cryptographic policy to DEFAULT."
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the configuration persists across reboots by setting the default policy in /etc/crypto-policies/config
echo "DEFAULT" | sudo tee /etc/crypto-policies/config > /dev/null

# Verify that the configuration file has been updated correctly
if grep -q "^DEFAULT$" /etc/crypto-policies/config; then
    echo "Configuration set to persist DEFAULT cryptographic policy across reboots."
else
    echo "Failed to configure persistent DEFAULT cryptographic policy."
    exit 1
fi

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure GnuTLS honors system-wide cryptographic policy
# This script configures GnuTLS to use the system crypto policy
# and disables legacy/insecure protocol versions.

# Define the path to the GnuTLS configuration file
GNUTLS_CONF="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"

# Check if the GnuTLS configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]]; then
  echo "GnuTLS configuration file not found: $GNUTLS_CONF"
  exit 1
fi

# Define the required settings for the GnuTLS configuration
REQUIRED_SETTINGS=(
  "DEFAULT_PRIORITY = \"SYSTEM\""
)

# Function to check if a setting is already present in the configuration file
setting_exists() {
  local setting="$1"
  grep -q "^$setting" "$GNUTLS_CONF"
}

# Apply the required settings
for setting in "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
  if ! setting_exists "$setting"; then
    echo "Adding setting to $GNUTLS_CONF: $setting"
    echo "$setting" >> "$GNUTLS_CONF"
  else
    echo "Setting already exists in $GNUTLS_CONF: $setting"
  fi
done

# Ensure the GnuTLS configuration file is owned by root and has the correct permissions
chown root:root "$GNUTLS_CONF"
chmod 644 "$GNUTLS_CONF"

# Reload the GnuTLS service to apply changes
systemctl reload gnutls-serv || true

echo "GnuTLS configuration has been updated to comply with system-wide cryptographic policy."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure GnuTLS honors system-wide cryptographic policy
# This script configures GnuTLS to use the system crypto policy
# and disables legacy/insecure protocol versions.

# Define the path to the GnuTLS configuration file
GNUTLS_CONF="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"

# Check if the GnuTLS configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]]; then
  echo "GnuTLS configuration file not found: $GNUTLS_CONF"
  exit 1
fi

# Define the required settings for the GnuTLS configuration
REQUIRED_SETTINGS=(
  "DEFAULT_PRIORITY = \"SYSTEM\""
)

# Function to check if a setting is already present in the configuration file
setting_exists() {
  local setting="$1"
  grep -q "^$setting" "$GNUTLS_CONF"
}

# Apply the required settings
for setting in "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
  if ! setting_exists "$setting"; then
    echo "Adding setting to $GNUTLS_CONF: $setting"
    echo "$setting" >> "$GNUTLS_CONF"
  else
    echo "Setting already exists in $GNUTLS_CONF: $setting"
  fi
done

# Ensure the GnuTLS configuration file is owned by root and has the correct permissions
chown root:root "$GNUTLS_CONF"
chmod 644 "$GNUTLS_CONF"

# Reload the GnuTLS service to apply changes
systemctl reload gnutls-serv || true

echo "GnuTLS configuration has been updated to comply with system-wide cryptographic policy."
```

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the SSH server uses the system-wide cryptographic policy
if ! grep -q "^Ciphers" /etc/ssh/sshd_config; then
    echo "Ciphers DEFAULT" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
else
    sed -i 's/^Ciphers .*/Ciphers DEFAULT/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
fi

# Ensure the system-wide cryptographic policy is set to FIPS 140-2
if ! update-crypto-policies --set FIPS; then
    echo "Failed to set crypto policy to FIPS"
    exit 1
fi

# Restart SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the SSH server's MAC algorithm selection conforms to the system-wide cryptographic policy

# Define the path to the sshd_config file
SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Backup the original sshd_config file if it hasn't been backed up yet
if [ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.backup" ]; then
    cp "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.backup"
fi

# Remove any existing MACs configuration in sshd_config
sed -i '/^MACs /d' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Append the directive to use system-wide cryptographic policy for MACs
echo "MACs +@sshd" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

# Restart the SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

# Ensure the SSH service starts on boot
systemctl enable sshd

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the SSH server's MAC algorithm selection conforms to the system-wide cryptographic policy

# Define the path to the sshd_config file
SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Backup the original sshd_config file if it hasn't been backed up yet
if [ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.backup" ]; then
    cp "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.backup"
fi

# Remove any existing MACs configuration in sshd_config

echo "MACs +@sshd" >> "${SSHD_CONFIG}"

systemctl enable sshd

echo "disable-user-list=true" >> /etc/dconf/db/gdm.d/01-hide-users

echo "file-db:/usr/local/share/gdm.d" >> /etc/dconf/profile/gdm

systemctl restart gdm

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the GNOME settings daemon is installed
if ! rpm -q gnome-settings-daemon; then
    echo "gnome-settings-daemon is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Create or update the dconf profile for system-wide settings
DCONF_PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/gdm"
echo "user-db:user" > "$DCONF_PROFILE"
echo "system-db:local" >> "$DCONF_PROFILE"

# Create the dconf local database directory if it doesn't exist
DCONF_LOCAL_DB="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCAL_DB"

# Define the dconf settings to lock the screen on smartcard removal
DCONF_SETTINGS="$DCONF_LOCAL_DB/01-lock-on-smartcard-removal"
cat <<EOF > "$DCONF_SETTINGS"
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=uint32 0

[org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]
logout=''
power-off=''
suspend=''
hibernate=''
EOF

# Update the dconf database
dconf update

# Ensure the settings are enforced by locking down the keys
DCONF_LOCK="$DCONF_LOCAL_DB/locks"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCK"

cat <<EOF > "$DCONF_LOCK/01-lock-on-smartcard-removal"
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/logout
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/power-off
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/suspend
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/hibernate
EOF

# Update the dconf database again to apply locks
dconf update

echo "System-wide policy set to lock the screen on smartcard removal."

echo "system-db:local" >> "$DCONF_PROFILE"

DCONF_LOCAL_DB="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCAL_DB"

# Define the dconf settings to lock the screen on smartcard removal
DCONF_SETTINGS="$DCONF_LOCAL_DB/01-lock-on-smartcard-removal"
cat <<EOF > "$DCONF_SETTINGS"
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=uint32 0

[org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys]
logout=''
power-off=''
suspend=''
hibernate=''
EOF

# Update the dconf database
dconf update

# Ensure the settings are enforced by locking down the keys
DCONF_LOCK="$DCONF_LOCAL_DB/locks"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCK"

cat <<EOF > "$DCONF_LOCK/01-lock-on-smartcard-removal"
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/logout
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/power-off
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/suspend
/org/gnome/settings-daemon/plugins/media-keys/hibernate
EOF

# Update the dconf database again to apply locks
dconf update

echo "System-wide policy set to lock the screen on smartcard removal."
```

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure GNOME Display Manager does not auto-login any user

# Check if GDM is installed
if ! rpm -q gdm > /dev/null; then
  echo "GNOME Display Manager (gdm) is not installed."
  exit 0
fi

# Backup the original configuration file
GDM_CONFIG_FILE="/etc/gdm/custom.conf"
BACKUP_FILE="${GDM_CONFIG_FILE}.backup"

if [ ! -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ]; then
  cp "$GDM_CONFIG_FILE" "$BACKUP_FILE"
fi

# Modify the GDM configuration to disable automatic login
sed -i '/^\[daemon\]/,/^\[/ {
  s/^AutomaticLoginEnable=.*/AutomaticLoginEnable=false/
  s/^AutomaticLogin=.*/AutomaticLogin=/
}' "$GDM_CONFIG_FILE"

# Ensure the changes are applied by restarting GDM
systemctl restart gdm

echo "Automatic graphical login has been disabled."

if ! rpm -q gnome-screensaver &> /dev/null; then

fi

# Enable and configure the screensaver to lock the screen on activation

echo "system-db:local" | sudo tee -a /etc/dconf/profile/user > /dev/null

cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/dconf/db/local.d/01-screensaver-lock > /dev/null
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=uint32 0
idle-activation-enabled=true
EOF

sudo dconf update

# Ensure the GNOME screensaver service is enabled and started
sudo systemctl enable --now gnome-screensaver.service

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure GNOME screensaver idle-lock setting is enforced and cannot be changed by users

# Create or update the dconf database to enforce the policy
dconf update-add /org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay 600 # Set lock delay to 10 minutes (600 seconds)
dconf update-add /org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/idle-activation-enabled true # Enable idle activation

# Make the settings read-only for all users
dconf update-add /org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay 's' # Ensure the lock delay is of type string
dconf update-add /org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/idle-activation-enabled 'b' # Ensure the idle activation is of type boolean

# Apply the changes immediately
dconf update

# Create a dconf profile to ensure settings persist across reboots
echo "[user]" > /etc/dconf/profile/user
echo "system-db:local" >> /etc/dconf/profile/user

# Create or update the local dconf database with the policy
mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks
echo "/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay" > /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/00-screensaver-lock-delay
echo "/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/idle-activation-enabled" >> /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/00-screensaver-idle-activation

# Update the dconf database to apply the locks
dconf update

# Ensure the script is idempotent by checking if the settings are already applied
current_lock_delay=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-delay)
current_idle_activation=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled)

if [[ "$current_lock_delay" -ne 600 || "$current_idle_activation" == "false" ]]; then
    echo "Settings were not correctly applied. Please review the script and try again."
    exit 1
fi

echo "GNOME screensaver idle-lock settings have been successfully enforced and locked down."

echo "system-db:local" >> /etc/dconf/profile/user

#!/bin/bash

# Define the desired idle delay in seconds (e.g., 600 seconds = 10 minutes)
IDLE_DELAY=600

# Ensure dconf is installed
if ! command -v dconf &> /dev/null; then
    echo "dconf could not be found, please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Create or update the dconf profile for system-wide settings
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/dconf/profile/user > /dev/null
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF

# Ensure the local dconf database directory exists
sudo mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks

# Set the idle delay and lock it to prevent user override
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/dconf/db/local.d/01-idle-delay > /dev/null
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=$IDLE_DELAY
EOF

cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/idle-delay > /dev/null
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
EOF

# Update the dconf database to apply changes
sudo dconf update

# Ensure that user settings cannot override system settings
sudo chmod 644 /etc/dconf/profile/user
sudo chmod -R 755 /etc/dconf/db/local.d/
sudo chown root:root /etc/dconf/profile/user
sudo chown -R root:root /etc/dconf/db/local.d/

# Restart the GNOME session to apply changes immediately (if applicable)
if pgrep gnome-session > /dev/null; then
    pkill -u $(whoami) gnome-shell
fi

if ! command -v dconf &> /dev/null; then
    echo "dconf could not be found, please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Create or update the dconf profile for system-wide settings
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/dconf/profile/user > /dev/null
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF

sudo mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks

cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/dconf/db/local.d/01-idle-delay > /dev/null

idle-delay=$IDLE_DELAY
EOF

# Define the GSettings key for disabling Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot
KEY="/org/gnome/desktop/session/lock-delay"
VALUE="uint32 0"

# Check if the current value is already set to disable Ctrl-Alt-Del
CURRENT_VALUE=$(gsettings get $KEY)

if [ "$CURRENT_VALUE" != "$VALUE" ]; then
    # Set the GSettings key to disable Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot
    gsettings set $KEY $VALUE

    # Lock the setting so that it cannot be overridden by individual users
    gsettings writable $KEY false
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Define the desired sudo timeout value in minutes
SUDO_TIMEOUT=5

# Ensure the /etc/sudoers file is backed up
if [ ! -f "/etc/sudoers.bak" ]; then
    cp /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.bak
fi

# Use visudo to safely edit the sudoers file and set the timeout
visudo -c <<EOF
Defaults timestamp_timeout=${SUDO_TIMEOUT}
EOF

# Check if the edit was successful
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "sudo timeout set to ${SUDO_TIMEOUT} minutes."
else
    echo "Failed to set sudo timeout. Reverting to backup."
    cp /etc/sudoers.bak /etc/sudoers
fi

# Remove the backup if the current configuration is correct
if visudo -c; then
    rm -f /etc/sudoers.bak
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Define the desired sudo timeout value in minutes
SUDO_TIMEOUT=5

# Ensure the /etc/sudoers file is backed up
if [ ! -f "/etc/sudoers.bak" ]; then
    cp /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.bak
fi

# Use visudo to safely edit the sudoers file and set the timeout
visudo -c <<EOF
Defaults timestamp_timeout=${SUDO_TIMEOUT}
EOF

# Check if the edit was successful
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "sudo timeout set to ${SUDO_TIMEOUT} minutes."
else
    echo "Failed to set sudo timeout. Reverting to backup."
    cp /etc/sudoers.bak /etc/sudoers
fi

# Remove the backup if the current configuration is correct
if visudo -c; then
    rm -f /etc/sudoers.bak
fi
